remediation.action: Inspect the CSS style property 'color' and the background color of the element
Check the color contrast using a tool like WebAIM's Color Contrast Checker or a Color Contrast Checker browser extension
Use the hex code #888 for the text color, which has a low contrast with the background
Replace the #333 hex code for the button's text color which currently has low contrast
Apply the required background and text color values in the CSS styles for the elements to achieve sufficient color contrast.

metadata.business_impact: The color contrast between text and background makes it difficult for users with visual impairments, including those who are legally blind or have low vision, to read the text on this page. This can cause eye strain and may prevent users from accessing important information.

violations.description: Ensures the order of headings is semantically correct
violations.help: Heading levels should only increase by one

any.message: Heading order invalid

nodes.failureSummary:
Fix any of the following:
  Heading order invalid

remediation.step: Check the current order of headings on the page.

metadata.business_impact: A person using a screen reader or other assistive technology may encounter difficulty navigating the content structure because headings do not follow a logical order. This can lead to confusion about the importance and grouping of related content.

remediation.step: Identify all <a> tags without text content
remediation.action: Locate the tags in the affected HTML, such as Facebook
Remove the <span> tags around the link text, making sure the text content is directly within the <a> tag

remediation.step: Verify that the link text is clear and concise
remediation.action: Ensure the link text accurately describes the link's destination and is descriptive enough for users to understand the purpose of the link

metadata.business_impact: This issue prevents screen reader users from understanding the purpose of a link, potentially causing confusion or disorientation. Additionally, users with low vision may have difficulty differentiating between different links if their text is not clear.

violations.description: Ensures all page content is contained by landmarks
violations.help: All page content should be contained by landmarks

any.message: Some page content is not contained by landmarks

nodes.failureSummary:
Fix any of the following:
  Some page content is not contained by landmarks

any.message: Some page content is not contained by landmarks

nodes.failureSummary:
Fix any of the following:
  Some page content is not contained by landmarks

metadata.friendly_name: Landmark Navigation Issue: Uncontained Link Content

remediation.step_1: Replace the <div> containing the link with a proper landmark element such as <main>, <nav>, or <footer> depending on its purpose on the page. In this case, we'll assume that it's the primary content and use <main>.
remediation.step_2:
Wrap the link in the new landmark element, like this:
New Candidate Registration
remediation.step_3: Review the rest of the page and ensure all other content is properly contained within landmarks, such as <header>, <nav>, <main>, and <footer> to maintain a clear structure and hierarchy.

metadata.business_impact: This issue makes it difficult for users who rely on screen readers to navigate the page as they need to understand the structure and hierarchy of the page content. Without landmarks, screen readers may not provide proper navigation cues, making it hard for users to find the link to New Candidate Registration.
